feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: US pledges to crack down on Haitian gangs making money off social media
entry.content: The U.S. envoy to the United Nations says the U.S. government plans to stop violent gangs in Haiti from profiting off social media

entry.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
entry.content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The early bird gets the worm, but the second mouse gets the cheese.
Lucky Numbers: 43, 34, 54, 70, 83, 79

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Keep a “Second Brain” Journal where you keep track of your goals, habits and top 3 daily moments.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: A man's beard grows fastest when he anticipates sex.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Franklin D. Roosevelt
entry.content: “Do Something. If it works, do more of it. If it doesn't, do something else.” — Franklin D. Roosevelt

entry.title: Ballon d’Or power rankings: Rodri, Messi, Harry Kane among favorites to win soccer's MVP after 2026 World Cup
entry.content: The 2026 Ballon d'Or field is wide open, and there are some heavy hitters in the mix to bring home the trophy.

entry.title: Colossal Biosciences reportedly in talks to raise new capital at $20B–$30B valuation
entry.content: The de-extinction startup is looking to double or triple its previous valuation, according to the report.

entry.title: Chargers could sign star free agent wide receiver and it's not Keenan Allen
entry.content: The Chargers could use another weapon or two, and there's a non-Keenan Allen free agent that would make a ton of sense.

entry.title: Amazon's Adaptive Display for Fire TVs is officially rolling out today
entry.content: Amazon's Adaptive Display accessibility feature for Fire TVs is officially rolling out today

entry.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
entry.content: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 best XI: SN picks all-star lineup, from Erling Haaland to Lionel Messi
entry.content: Spain may have triumphed at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but this summer's tournament was all about the star players who showed up in droves.

entry.title: President Trump vows revenge on Iran for killing U.S. service members
entry.content: After a weekend of deadly attacks, the Pentagon has released the identities of some of the fallen service members, including 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan from Hawaii and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales from Texas, who were killed from an Iranian attack. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports from the region.

entry.title: LeBron James update leaves fans in agony
entry.content: LeBron James is sure taking his time to make his free agency decision.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts second round predictions and previews
entry.content: There are several mouth-watering matchups set for the round of 16.

entry.title: Why NFL players want grass fields equal to World Cup quality — but won't get them in 2026
entry.content: Here's the rundown on why many around the NFL want better and more widespread grass fields instead of turf.

entry.title: LIV Golf lawsuit, explained: Why tech company is suing golf league in latest legal issue
entry.content: Here's more on the latest in LIV Golf's rocky summer and it's looming financial troubles

entry.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
entry.content: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

entry.title: Pistons' Cade Cunningham's New Sneaker Gets an Official Release Date
entry.content: Cade Cunningham of the Detroit Pistons has a new Nike dropping in August.

entry.title: The first UL 3700-compliant plug-in solar microinverter is now available in the US
entry.content: It's a step toward making alternative energy accessible on a smaller, renter-friendly scale.

entry.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
entry.content: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

entry.title: FDA still probing Taylor Farms lettuce despite false positive test
entry.content: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing" in the cyclosporiasis outbreak.

entry.title: Trump to impose 50% tariffs on Canadian hockey sticks, alcohol and other goods
entry.content: President Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on a range of Canadian goods, from hockey equipment to alcoholic beverages, escalating the trade dispute between the two countries.

entry.title: Army veteran sets off fireworks, starts fire outside NYC federal building, FBI says
entry.content: A man was arrested after law enforcement officials said he ignited fireworks, poured flammable liquid and started a fire at 26 Federal Plaza in Lower Manhattan.

entry.title: Funeral held for Nolan Wells as investigation into his death continues
entry.content: Family and friends gathered on Monday to pay their final respects to Nolan Wells, the Mississippi teen who was found dead after a Fourth of July boating trip. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry has the details.

entry.title: New details on Manhattan explosion suspect
entry.content: The FBI has identified the suspect in Monday morning's explosion outside a federal building in New York City as a 43-year-old U.S. Army veteran. CBS News' Anna Schecter has the latest.

entry.title: Iran Missile Hit U.S. Troop Housing in Jordan
entry.content: Plus, a judge temporarily blocks the Paramount-Warner deal, and Tom Hiddleston studied Latin to read graffiti at Pompeii.

entry.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
entry.content: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

entry.title: U.S. launches 10th night of strikes on Iran after 3 U.S. troops killed
entry.content: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

entry.title: What to know about Taylor Farms amid the cyclospora outbreak
entry.content: The nation's largest recent cyclospora outbreak has put one of America's biggest produce suppliers under renewed scrutiny.

entry.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
entry.content: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is one of the basic building blocks of AI interoperability, giving AI models a secure way to access external data sources and services. It’s the plumbing that lets a chatbot reach into your calendar, your database, or your internal tools, instead of engineers building custom pipes for every connection. Next […]

entry.title: Nearly 100 U.S. service members injured in Iranian strikes this month
entry.content: Almost 100 American service members have been injured in strikes on bases in the Middle East this month, U.S. officials said.

entry.title: FDA retracts positive cyclospora test on Taylor Farms lettuce
entry.content: The FDA says the test identifying cyclospora in Taylor Farms iceberg lettuce was a "false positive." But the lettuce is still linked epidemiologically to cases of the illness in a multistate outbreak.

entry.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
entry.content: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

entry.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
entry.content: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

entry.title: Natural raises $30M to reinvent payments for AI agents — and take on Stripe
entry.content: The one-year-old startup aims to reinvent financial architecture for autonomous AI transactions.

entry.title: Tate brothers in Miami court after new U.K. charges
entry.content: Andrew and Tristan Tate appeared in a Miami courtroom after British prosecutors unveiled a wave of new charges, including rape. Authorities from the U.K. are seeking to extradite the Tate brothers. NBC News' Danny Cevallos reports on the 38 charges they face.

entry.title: Tropical depression nearing U.S. Gulf Coast set to become a tropical storm, forecasters say
entry.content: A tropical depression drifting erratically in the northern Gulf of Mexico is expected to strengthen later Monday with rain that could trigger flooding and winds capable of downing trees and power lines in several states this week.

entry.title: Capital One bet big on Discover. Now it must prove the gamble was worth it
entry.content: Tuesday evening's second-quarter earnings report is the perfect stage to do just that.

entry.title: Top Seahawks storylines going into training camp 2026
entry.content: What is going to stand out the most for the Seahawks this summer?

entry.title: Barcelona veteran’s exit could be announced on Tuesday or Wednesday – report
entry.content: Marc-Andre ter Stegen continues to train with FC Barcelona as he waits for the final details of his loan move to Ajax to be completed.According to MARCA, the German goalkeeper took part in Monday’s ...

entry.title: Rick Shiels gives his honest opinion on Bryson DeChambeau’s rules violation at The Open
entry.content: The Bryson DeChambeau fiasco at The Open Championship on Friday has certainly divided opinion. DeChambeau caused serious uproar at The Open last week when he was penalized two strokes for breaking the rules and subsequently threatened to withdraw from the championship.

entry.title: Judge pauses $110B Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
entry.content: The lawsuit from the states alleges that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

entry.title: Browns QB Shedeur Sanders opens up on relationship with Deshaun Watson
entry.content: While Shedeur Sanders and Deshaun Watson are deadlocked in a battle for the Browns' starting job, they're taking their relationship in stride

entry.title: Spain Won Its First Men’s World Cup Since 2010. Here’s How the Team’s Jerseys Brought Meaning to the Field
entry.content: What to know about player’s uniforms, from its nod to their names to the motif representing their previous FIFA World Cup win

entry.title: FDA says it’s still investigating Taylor Farms in cyclosporiasis outbreak
entry.content: Taylor Farms continues to be the subject of a Food and Drug Administration investigation into the growing cyclosporiasis outbreak, despite a false positive test result, the agency said Monday.

entry.title: Pre-season is picking up steam: Here’s how the BVB Women’s training camp is going
entry.content: Upcoming dates for the BVB Women at training camp21 July22 July 10:00: Warm-up in Westendorf23 JulyThe best photos from training camp

entry.title: Schaffran: “Professional debuts are the measure of our work”
entry.content: Paul Schaffran on the current start to the 2026/27 season: “Some teams are still in the process of leaving the club. At the same time, however, the U17, U19, and U23 competitive teams have resumed t...

entry.title: Pat Riley Addresses Miami Heat’s Chances of Signing LeBron James
entry.content: Pat Riley and the Miami Heat want to sign LeBron James. After trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo, Riley said there is […]

entry.title: Judge says Paramount and Warner must halt merger for at least two weeks, granting states' request
entry.content: A federal judge on Monday ordered Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their $81 billion merger for at least two weeks, allowing states that are challenging the deal more time to see their case through in court.

entry.title: Judge puts temporary pause on Paramount-Warner merger
entry.content: Paramount Skydance’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery hit a roadblock Monday after a federal judge temporarily paused the proposed merger.

entry.title: Ex-Olympian charged with damaging Reflecting Pool gets Sept. 28 trial date
entry.content: A judge in the nation’s capital has set a September trial date for a former Olympic canoe racer charged with deliberately damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool

entry.title: The men behind Trump's $3.47 gas: An NFL coach, a GOP fundraiser and two New Jersey brothers
entry.content: President Donald Trump and his White House have enthusiastically promoted the Freedom Fuel Network, a chain of star-spangled convenience stores selling gas at $3.47 per gallon in honor of the 47th president.

entry.title: Political analysts see little chance of GOP renewing Planned Parenthood funding freeze
entry.content: Political strategists on both sides of the abortion debate say there's little chance of Congress renewing a Trump administration freeze on federal funding to Planned Parenthood that expired this month.

entry.title: Palantir isn’t the only option for the NHS | Letter
entry.content: Manchester’s integrated care board offers the health service a domestic alternative to the US firm, says Dr Devan MoodleyRegarding Peter Geoghegan’s article (I investigated Palantir’s foothold in the British state – and what I found should worry us all, 15 July), the nine years that Greater Manchester’s health system has spent without Palantir is a result not of caution but bold conviction.Greater Manchester’s integrated care board remains the only one in England to decline the Palantir-built federated data platform, having concluded that its own homegrown data capability was stronger. It built that capability with British universities, NHS clinicians and domestic technology firms. Continue reading...

entry.title: Trial begins for Massachusetts mom who says postpartum issues led her to kill 3 children
entry.content: Jury selection began Monday in the trial of a Massachusetts mother who strangled her three children while undergoing medical treatment for psychiatric problems that developed after she gave birth.

entry.title: Anti-ICE suspect arrested outside New York City federal building after lighting incendiary device
entry.content: Three people, including two firefighters, were injured Monday and a suspected anti-ICE activist hauling weapons was arrested after he lit an "incendiary device" outside a Manhattan building that houses immigration enforcement, the FBI and other federal agencies, federal officials said.

entry.title: Pamela Smart, serving life, seeks a new trial as a New Hampshire judge weighs her latest petition
entry.content: Lawyers for Pamela Smart, who is serving life in prison for orchestrating the murder of her husband by her teenage student in 1990, urged a New Hampshire judge Monday to allow her latest petition for a new trial to move forward.

entry.title: 3 women's world records set at the annual Lumberjack World Championships in Wisconsin
entry.content: Three new women's world records have been set at the Lumberjack World Championships competition held in a small Wisconsin community, full of high-powered sawing, chopping, logrolling, speed climbing and cheers of "Yo-Ho!"

entry.title: Ex-Mexican drug lord Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada denounces violence as he's sentenced to life in prison
entry.content: Former Mexican drug kingpin Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada was sentenced in a U.S. court on Monday to life in prison and called for an end to the brand of brutal violence he used for decades to grow the Sinaloa cartel into the world's largest drug trafficking organization.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: AMD, Archer Aviation, SpaceX, Iren & more
entry.content: These are the stocks posting the largest moves in midday trading.

entry.title: Pamela Smart, serving life, seeks a new trial
entry.content: Lawyers for Pamela Smart, who's serving life for orchestrating her husband's 1990 murder, are hoping that a New Hampshire judge will allow her latest petition for a new trial

entry.title: Braiden Shaw says AI is this generation's 'one window' to build wealth — but warns time to capitalize is running out
entry.content: Braiden Shaw says AI is this generation's 'one window' to build wealth — but warns time to capitalize is running out

entry.title: BlackRock Leads $12 Billion Financing for New Meta Data Centers in Texas
entry.content: The asset manager’s infrastructure and private-credit arms are leading the jumbo project.

entry.title: Tempus AI Scoops Up Personalis For $1.5 Billion; But Shares Dive
entry.content: Tempus AI Scoops Up Personalis For $1.5 Billion; But Shares Dive

entry.title: Gold prices today, Monday, July 20, 2026: Gold holds above $4,000 despite continued violence in the Middle East
entry.content: Gold prices today, Monday, July 20, 2026: Gold holds above $4,000 despite continued violence in the Middle East

entry.title: Oystercatchers, known for sharp bills and beady eyes, are making a comeback
entry.content: A shorebird known for its sharp, orange bill and beady eyes is making a comeback in North America thanks to a yearslong conservation effort

entry.title: Why Rockies aren't likely to trade Hunter Goodman to Yankees
entry.content: Goodman may be staying put in Colorado.

entry.title: Jonathan Kuminga's free agency all ties into Cavs, Lakers and LeBron James
entry.content: Everything in the NBA world is connected.

entry.title: Mariners trade idea uses George Kirby to land .795 OPS utilityman and 3.18 ERA RP from Giants
entry.content: This would be a bold and risky trade idea from the Mariners.

entry.title: Get tickets to WNBA All-Star 2026: Cheapest prices, schedule, rosters for events in Chicago
entry.content: Tickets to WNBA All-Star weekend are available now! Here's how to buy seats for the two-day event in Chicago.

entry.title: Shakira concert tickets: How to buy, presale prices, schedule for extended 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran' Tour 2026
entry.content: Shakira continues her 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran' Tour in 2026 with a handful of shows across the U.S. Here's how to score tickets.

entry.title: Here's the truth about Francisco Lindor, Juan Soto relationship with Mets
entry.content: Lindor is involved in trade rumors as the deadline approaches.

entry.title: Michigan athletics investigation finds 'weaknesses', changes promised
entry.content: The Jenner & Block law firm, which U-M brought in seven months back to investigate its athletic department culture, has completed its report.

entry.title: Lydia Ko talks to fellow Kiwi Ryan Fox after his British Open triumph
entry.content: Ko opened the call with, "Hello, Champion Golfer of the Year," pumping the air with her fist.

entry.title: Michigan AD Warde Manuel agrees to buyout, will step down at end of 2026
entry.content: The University of Michigan and embattled athletic director Warde Manuel have agreed to a buyout of the remaining years on his contract and he will step down at the end of the year, culminating a decade-long run with four national championships and a string of scandals. The school said Monday the decision was tied to investigations into former football coach Sherrone Moore's relationship with his executive assistant and a probe into the athletic department's culture. Manuel led a department behind this year's men's basketball title and a football program that won the 2023 national championship, but that wasn't enough for him to keep his job.

entry.title: Why Michigan AD Warde Manuel will step down at end of year after 'internal investigation'
entry.content: After changes atop both the football and basketball programs, Michigan will soon hire a new athletic director.

entry.title: A guide to 2026 WNBA All-Star weekend: Schedule, coaches, rosters
entry.content: The 2026 WNBA All-Star Game will feature a mix of elite veterans and young superstars at the United Center in Chicago on Saturday.

entry.title: ❌No party, no bank holiday: Argentina won't go to Casa Rosada
entry.content: Without a doubt, it is a very hard day for all Argentinians after yesterday’s defeat against Spain. And the National Team has made a new decision for the coming hours. As early as last night, rumors...

entry.title: SEC football media days will return to Nashville in 2027
entry.content: SEC commissioner Greg Sankey announced that Nashville will host next year's SEC football media days on July 12-15, 2027.

entry.title: UFC Abu Dhabi: How to watch Ankalaev vs. Guskov, lineup, odds, more
entry.content: Here's what you need to know to watch UFC Fight Night 282 on Paramount+.

entry.title: ‘There has been talk…’ – Liverpool touted to potentially ruin Man Utd’s transfer plans for 25y/o
entry.content: One transfer insider has claimed that Liverpool could potentially scupper Manchester United’s plans to bring in a midfielder who impressed at the World Cup.When the Merseysiders were going through a...

entry.title: NVIDIA data center hardware is being cooled with water 'hotter than a hot tub'
entry.content: It may seem counterintuitive, but 113-degree water is still cool enough to cool NVIDA's latest hardware.

entry.title: AliExpress hit with record $629 million fine for selling counterfeit and illegal products
entry.content: Europe just hit AliExpress with a record $629 million fine for allowing the sale of counterfeit and harmful products.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts: Draw, schedule, prize money and format explained
entry.content: One of the biggest majors of the year is here!

entry.title: Meta Glasses review: A bit less polish, a lot more baggage
entry.content: Meta's glasses deliver the same functionality as its Ray-Ban models but don't quite feel as premium.

entry.title: Iran War Mediators Push for Traction on a New Ceasefire
entry.content: Regional powers are worried about rising economic damage and the risk of escalation, but still face the challenge of getting the combatants on board.

entry.title: Why Spain cropped Donald Trump out of World Cup celebration photos
entry.content: While the U.S. president was initially on stage for Spain's celebrations, the team used a picture without Trump on social media.

entry.title: The Data Nerd Inheriting Washington’s Radioactive Numbers Job
entry.content: Brett Matsumoto spent a decade in the trenches of how the government measures the economy. Now he must convince Americans the stats can still be trusted.

entry.title: Bills' Keon Coleman takes spotlight as World Cup ends
entry.content: The enigmatic wide receiver has a point to prove.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 winners and losers: Spain and Africa show out, Portugal and USMNT flounder
entry.content: The future looks bright within Spain's ranks following its 1-0 victory over Argentina in the World Cup final. Here's why.

entry.title: Gas prices rise above $4, on average, for the first time since mid-June
entry.content: The average U.S. price of gas rose above $4 per gallon on Monday as fighting in the oil-rich Middle East flares up.

entry.title: Yaxel Lendeborg's Warriors pitch to LeBron James was right
entry.content: Lendeborg got a bit of pushback from Draymond Green.

entry.title: China's new AI model halts new subscriptions as demand swamps capacity
entry.content: The Chinese AI model Kimi K3 has suspended new subscriptions after overwhelming demand pushed its capacity close to its limits

entry.title: Lakers urged to sign $45M disruptive 3-and-D veteran regardless of Jonathan Kuminga's decision
entry.content: The Lakers are going after Jonathan Kuminga. However, they have been called on to sign a veteran free agent that would be massive help.

entry.title: He Bought a House ‘Sight Unseen.’ Then He Found Three Dead Bodies.
entry.content: Skeletal remains were discovered inside a Connecticut home sold through a foreclosure auction—an extreme example of what buyers can encounter when purchasing homes this way.

entry.title: China’s Campaign to Suffocate Taiwan Is Hitting Children’s Choirs and Baristas
entry.content: Beijing has gotten creative in its continuing efforts to undermine the island’s international standing.

entry.title: Who will replace Lu Dort in the Thunder's starting lineup?
entry.content: OKC has some great options to fill out their starters
